heroTitle: Governed automation for operations that cannot afford chaos.
heroLead: Nexa audits your current stack, maps operational risk, and identifies the first lane worth replacing. Then we carry that plan into a controlled deployment path for collections, compliance, handoff queues, and document-heavy workflows.
funnelSubline: Audit → Design → Replace. Your stack, your pace, your control.
problemHeadline: Most automation breaks at the edges that matter most.
problemBody: Money movement, legal exposure, customer handoff, and document routing fail when teams stitch together tools they do not control. Nexa replaces those brittle lanes in stages so operators keep visibility and override power from day one.
phaseOneBody: We map the current stack, hidden manual work, and where automation or replacement could create operational risk. You get a deployment map and the first lane worth shipping.
phaseTwoBody: We define the control plane, data boundaries, review steps, and human override paths before anything touches production. The user journey is tuned for the realities of your workflow.
phaseThreeBody: We ship one governed lane at a time through the Nexa platform so you replace brittle operations without a cliff migration or trust collapse.
journeySteps: Receive your map and first recommended lane in 48 hours.
Join our SaaS for phase-by-phase replacement with support and design.
auditCtaBody: One brief. We return a fit assessment, risk notes, and the first governed lane worth deploying.
saasCtaBody: Our platform turns the audit into working deployment: launch surface, intake flow, workflow engine, operator view, and the safety rails needed to keep it under control.
saasCtaButton: Start the audit to get access
intakeBody: Tell us what you run today. We return a deployment map, risk notes, and the first automation lane worth shipping.
successBody: Your company profile is in the queue. Nexa will return a fit assessment and deployment direction within 48 hours.
proofBody: Nexa is designed for collections, compliance, customer operations, and other high-consequence workflows where auditability, constrained automation, and human override are non-negotiable.

systemCards.title: Revenue Infrastructure
systemCards.body: Lead capture, qualification, follow-up, quoting, payment links, and collections handoff in one operating surface.

systemCards.title: Backoffice Automation
systemCards.body: Document routing, intake classification, customer operations, status updates, and repeatable internal workflows.

systemCards.title: Governed AI Layers
systemCards.body: Human override, audit logs, constrained automation, and system boundaries designed before production launch.

deliverySteps.title: Assess
deliverySteps.body: Map current tools, hidden manual work, and where risk appears when automation touches customers or money.

deliverySteps.title: Architect
deliverySteps.body: Define control plane, data boundaries, review gates, and Vercel-facing product surfaces.

deliverySteps.title: Ship
deliverySteps.body: Launch the first working lane fast: landing page, intake flow, workflow engine, or operator dashboard.

deliverySteps.title: Stabilize
deliverySteps.body: Add monitoring, escalation paths, incident handling, and the next automations without losing control.

rails: Consent-first automation and verified user paths
Manual override for high-risk steps and customer-facing actions
Separation between frontend speed and sensitive backend logic
Security and ethics review for systems that touch money or legal exposure
footerNote: Built for Canadian deployment with governed automation, operator control, and no dependence on generic SaaS lock-in.
launchSurfaceTitle: Start with a serious public surface. Then wire the operating system behind it.
launchSurfaceBody: Nexa begins with a clean acquisition layer, but the point is not a prettier landing page. The point is to connect trust, intake, workflows, operator review, and deployment discipline into one coherent system.
formFootnote: Endpoints can be overridden at build time with NEXT_PUBLIC_ACCESS_ENDPOINT, NEXT_PUBLIC_LEAD_ENDPOINT, and NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT.
